# minio_notify_postgres (Data Source)



## Example Usage

```terraform
data "minio_notify_postgres" "example" {
  name = "my-postgres"
}

output "postgres_table" {
  value = data.minio_notify_postgres.example.table
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Target name identifier.

### Read-Only

- `comment` (String) Comment or description for this notification target.
- `connection_string` (String) PostgreSQL connection string (e.g., 'host=localhost port=5432 dbname=minio user=minio password=secret sslmode=disable'). MinIO does not return this value on read; Terraform keeps the value from your configuration.
- `enable` (Boolean) Whether this notification target is enabled.
- `format` (String) Output format for event records: 'namespace' or 'access'.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `max_open_connections` (Number) Maximum number of open connections to the PostgreSQL database.
- `queue_dir` (String) Directory path for persistent event store when the target is offline.
- `queue_limit` (Number) Maximum number of undelivered messages to queue.
- `restart_required` (Boolean) Indicates whether a MinIO server restart is required.
- `table` (String) Name of the PostgreSQL table for event records.
